Details: Removal of perfluorooctane sulfonate (PFOS) from water using hydrogen peroxide was found to be ineffective in one study [2160]. The bench-scale study achieved 2 percent PFOS removal with an H2O2 dose of 3.0 mg/L. The same study showed a Fenton's reagent test achieved no removal utilizing 500 mg/L FeSO4 in combination with 5 mg/L H2O2. The lack of removal was thought to be due to the chemical structure of PFOS. Perfluorinated surfactants are designed to resist heat and reducing/oxidizing agents.
